title: Luxury Lounge Layout Planner
description: The user acts as an interior architect arranging a luxury lounge space. They place and configure velvet sofas and coffee mugs on side tables to satisfy a client brief with a fixed furniture budget. Every placement or material change costs budget points, enforcing a conservation law, and all actions support full undo/redo.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (importmap with ES modules from cdn.jsdelivr.net). Load coffeeMug.glb and GlamVelvetSofa.glb via GLTFLoader. Scene: a 12x12 meter floor plane with a subtle marble texture (procedural or checkerboard), soft ambient light (0.4 intensity), one warm directional light casting shadows, and an HDR-toned background color (#1a1a2e). Use OrbitControls for camera navigation, starting at a 45-degree elevated angle.

Core mechanic — Budget Conservation Law: The user starts with exactly 100 budget points. Placing a sofa costs 20 points; placing a mug costs 5 points. Changing a sofa's velvet color costs 3 points. Rotating any object costs 1 point. Removing an item refunds its original placement cost. The budget display (top-right HUD) must always reflect the correct total; points are strictly conserved.

Interactions (10-14 steps expected): (1-2) Click 'Add Sofa' button to spawn a GlamVelvetSofa at a random valid floor position, up to 4 sofas. (3-4) Click 'Add Mug' to spawn a coffeeMug on a small procedural side-table disc near the most recently placed sofa. (5-6) Click any placed sofa to select it (outline highlight), then use a color picker (5 preset swatches: burgundy, navy, emerald, charcoal, ivory) to change its material color. (7-8) Use rotation slider (0-360°) to rotate the selected object around Y-axis. (9-10) Right-click an object to remove it and reclaim budget. (11-12) Press Ctrl+Z for undo and Ctrl+Y for redo — maintain a full action history stack that correctly reverses/reapplies budget changes, placements, color changes, and rotations. (13-14) A 'Validate Layout' button checks that at least 2 sofas and 2 mugs are placed with remaining budget ≥ 0, showing a success or failure toast.

Multi-input coupling: rotating a sofa also repositions its associated mug to stay beside it. Display object count, budget, and last-action label in a minimal dark-themed HUD overlay. All objects must cast and receive shadows.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  budgetPoints: number,         // remaining budget points (starts at 100)
  sofaCount: number,         // total sofas currently placed in scene
  mugCount: number,         // total coffee mugs currently placed
  totalRotations: number,         // cumulative count of all rotation actions performed
  colorChanges: number,         // total sofa color changes applied
  selectedSofaIndex: number,         // index of currently selected sofa (-1 if none)
  refundedPoints: number,         // cumulative budget points refunded from removals
  placementAttempts: number,         // total placement button clicks (sofas + mugs)
  isLayoutValid: boolean,         // true if at least 2 sofas and 2 mugs placed
  averageSofaSpacing: number,         // mean distance between all sofa pairs in meters
}
